steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function has the form $$f(x) = mx + b,$$ where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the $y$-intercept. The slope is given as $51$, so $m=51$.

steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content: The graph passes through $(0,0)$, so the $y$-intercept is $b=0$. Substituting: $$f(x) = 51x + 0 = 51x.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $f(x)=51x$ — slope 51, passes through origin. Matches both conditions.
  • B) $f(x)=34x$ — slope is 34, not 51. Eliminated.
  • C) $f(x)=17x$ — slope is 17, not 51. Eliminated.
  • D) $f(x)=x$ — slope is 1, not 51. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
